ogTitle: Warding East vs Warnbro: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Warding East and Warnbro.
heading: Warding East vs Warnbro — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Warding East, Western Australia and Warnbro, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Warding East has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Warnbro (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Warding East or Warnbro safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Warding East has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Warnbro scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Warding East scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Warnbro scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Warding East or Warnbro?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Warding East has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
